claim: For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1, n + 1) divides 2.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1, n + 1) is at most 2.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1, n + 1) equals gcd(2,n+1).
For all even integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1, n + 1) equals 1.
For all odd integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1, n + 1) equals 2.
For all even integers n, n^2 + 1 and n + 1 are coprime.
For all odd integers n, (n^2 + 1)/2 and (n + 1)/2 are coprime.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1)^2 divides 4.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) equals 1 exactly when n is even.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) equals 2 exactly when n is odd.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) divides n^2 - 1.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) divides n^4 - 1.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) divides n^3 + 1.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) equals gcd(n^2 + 1,2).
For all integers n, phi(gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1)) equals 1.
For all integers n, tau(gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1)) is at most 2.
For all integers n, sigma(gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1)) is at most 3.
For all odd integers n, vp(n^2 + 1,2) is at least 1.
For all odd integers n, vp(n^2 + 1,2) equals vp(n + 1,2) or exceeds it.
For all odd integers n, n^2 + 1 is congruent to 2 modulo 8.
For all even integers n, n^2 + 1 is odd.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) equals gcd(n - 1,n + 1).
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) equals gcd(n - 1,2).
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1,n - 1) equals 1.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) divides sigma(abs(n)+1).
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) divides tau(abs(n)+1).
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) divides phi(abs(n)+1).
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) divides n^5 + 1.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) divides n^7 + 1.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) divides n^6 - 1.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) divides n^8 - 1.
For all even integers n, n^2 + n + 2 is odd.
For all odd integers n, n^2 + n + 2 is even.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) equals gcd(n^2 - 1,n + 1).
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) equals abs(n+1) when abs(n+1) divides 2.
predicate: ((2 % abs(n+1) != 0) if (n+1)!=0 else False) or (gcd(n**2 + 1,n+1) == abs(n+1))

claim: For all integers n, any prime dividing gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) is equal to 2.
predicate: all((not is_prime(p)) or (gcd(n**2 + 1,n+1) % p != 0) or (p == 2) for p in range(2,abs(gcd(n**2 + 1,n+1))+1))

claim: For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) is a power of 2.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) divides n^2 + n + 2.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) divides n^3 + n^2 + n + 1.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) divides n^4 + 1.
For all integers n, sigma(gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1)) equals gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1)+1.
For all integers n, tau(gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1)) equals gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1).
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) divides factorial(gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1)+1).
For all odd integers n, n^2 + 1 is divisible by gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1)^2.
For all even integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1)^2 divides n^2 + 1.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) divides n^10 - 1.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) divides n^9 + 1.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) equals 2 if and only if phi(abs(n)+1) is even.
For all odd integers n, phi(n^2 + 1) is even.
For all even integers n, phi(n^2 + 1) is even whenever abs(n^2+1)>2.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) divides sigma(abs(n^2 + 1)).
For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1,n + 1) divides sigma(abs(n + 1)).
